title: The End of Your Sixth Year
prose: The last night of sixth year doesn't feel like the ones before it. There's no real ceremony to it — just the ordinary packing, the ordinary train — but conversations trail off half-finished more often than usual, and more than one owl arrives at breakfast carrying news nobody reads aloud.
Whatever seventh year turns out to be, it won't be quite what any of the years before it were.

choices.label: Go home for the summer, and wait to see what September brings.
